body.title: CC inbox consumer: /readinbox skill + context-load auto-read
body.result: /readinbox skill + context-load Step 5.6 unread check + 'you've got mail' pattern documented. End-to-end sanity verified (append → list → mark-all-read flow). 6c1c3101.
body.description:
Build the CC-side consumer: (1) a /readinbox skill that displays unread entries summarized; (2) a context-load step addition that reads unread on session start so CC surfaces Igor notifications up front; (3) a 'you've got mail' query-hook pattern documented in the skill so CC peeks at inbox on every user prompt and surfaces unread before answering. Mark-read happens when surfaced.

**Affected files:** new ~/.claude/skills/readinbox/SKILL.md, ~/.claude/skills/context-load/SKILL.md (add step), lab/claudecode/cc_inbox.py (already has read_unread/mark_read from T-cc-inbox-storage)
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not (no tests because: skill-level behavior, tested through manual invocation + observation)
**Scope boundary:** IN — the skill files + context-load wiring. OUT — producer side (T-cc-inbox-producer), email bridge (future).
**Test plan:** manual — post a fake entry via cc_inbox.append(), invoke /readinbox, verify summary displays, verify entry marked read after display. Follow-up observation: does the 'on every user prompt' pattern get me to actually check? If not, iterate.
